expect: Introducing invented concepts into 3 frontier-depleted domains (security, governance, filtering) generates >=2 new frontier questions per domain. Falsification: <2 of 3 domains produce genuinely new frontiers.
actual: 6 new frontier questions generated across 3 depleted domains (2/2/2). All 3 domains produced genuinely new frontiers. 4 invented concepts transferred: epistemic-lock (2 uses), goodhart-cascade (1), sensor-only-trap (1), diagnosis-repair-gap (1), vocabulary-ceiling (1). Each question includes F-INV2 provenance tag explaining why the question couldn't be asked without the transferred concept.
diff: Expected >=2 per domain: CONFIRMED at 2/2/2 = 6 total. Target domains changed from initial (game-theory, distributed-systems, operations-research had active frontiers) to truly depleted (security, governance, filtering with 0 active each). Key finding: concept transfer was easier than expected — each depleted domain had clear vocabulary gaps that mapped directly to invented concepts. The epistemic-lock concept was most transferable (2 domains). Self-referential test: F-FLT5 uses vocabulary-ceiling to ask whether filtering vocabulary is ceiling-limited — recursive self-application.

method.selection_criteria: Domains with 0 active frontiers AND >=2 resolved frontiers (rich knowledge base but exhausted inquiry space)

vocabulary_ceiling_evidence.security: Prior questions: 'is it secure?' and 'does correction work?' — never questioned evidence quality or metric contamination
vocabulary_ceiling_evidence.governance: Prior questions: 'can we detect?' and 'can we decide?' — never questioned detection→repair conversion or decision→outcome pipeline
vocabulary_ceiling_evidence.filtering: Prior questions: all measurement (selectivity, scale, cascades, monitoring) — never questioned measurement bias or design vocabulary absence

new_frontiers.title: Does correction rate Goodhart into correction quality?
Is governance monitoring a sensor-only trap?
Does diagnosis-repair gap apply to council decisions?
Is filtering domain trapped in measurement vocabulary?
Is cascade independence finding epistemically locked?

falsification_assessment.criterion: <2 of 3 domains produce genuinely new frontiers
falsification_assessment.result: NOT FALSIFIED — 3/3 domains produced 2 genuinely new frontiers each
falsification_assessment.genuineness_test: Each frontier includes F-INV2 provenance tag explaining why the question requires the transferred concept. Prior domain vocabulary could not formulate these questions.
caveats: Frontier generation is the easy part — adoption (do future sessions actually work on these?) is the real test
n=3 domains is small — need replication across more depleted domains
Self-assessment of 'genuinely new' may be biased — need external evaluation

meta_observation: Depleted domains have a common vocabulary ceiling pattern: they exhaust MEASUREMENT questions (what exists? does it work? how much?) before hitting DESIGN questions (how to build new? what if evidence is wrong?). Concept-inventor vocabulary breaks the ceiling by providing meta-concepts (epistemic-lock, goodhart-cascade) that question the evidence quality of resolved findings — turning closure into re-opening.
